title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: folder segmented minirhizotron images want depth-resolved root traits one call — without writing loops — root_depth_metrics() right starting point. handles file loading, depth-map construction, per-bin zonal statistics, derived metrics, fault tolerance, optional file output. toggle metric groups compute; everything else taken care . line--line explanation underlying steps, see Minirhizotron Scans vignette. multiple images come tube, consider stitching beforehand Image Stitching

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: Images three directories must alphabetical order ; position corresponds physical tube/window.

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("jcunow/RootScanR")  library(RootScanR) library(tidyverse)   # for downstream plotting

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: fastest way get going. path.seg required; everything else sensible defaults. path.skl optional — omitted, skeletons needed length, diameter, angle metrics computed internally via skeletonize_image(). result data frame one row per tube × depth-bin combination. Always-present columns: Tube, Session, Plot, depth. Default metric columns: rootpx, voidpx, rootlength, avg.diameter, max.diameter, var.diameter, rootpx.density, rootlength.density, mrd, rpi, total.length.density, rootlength.fraction, mean.var.diameter, rootsurface_rootvolume_ratio.
code: result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   # optional — computed internally if omitted   session  = "2022_02" )  head(result)

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: tubes differ insertion angle soil-surface position, supply vector values (one per image). accept single recycled value images share setting. tube names follow different convention default (characters 3–5 right filename), always supply tube_names explicitly.
code: # Load your field metadata table tube_meta <- read.csv("metadata/tube_metadata_2022_02.csv") # Expected columns: filename, angle, soil_row, tube_id  result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg         = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl         = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   insertion_angles = tube_meta$angle,       # degrees from vertical   soil_starts      = tube_meta$soil_row,    # pixel row of soil surface   tube_names       = tube_meta$tube_id,     # e.g. "T042"   session          = "2022_02",   dpi              = 300 )

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: metric group logical argument. Disabled groups produce NA columns rather absent columns, downstream code can always reference column names regardless computed.
code: result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg  = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl  = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   path.rgb  = "scans/blended/2022_02/",   session   = "2022_02",    # --- core (on by default, shown explicitly for clarity) ---   calc_root_pixels    = TRUE,   calc_root_length    = TRUE,   calc_diameter_stats = TRUE,    # --- derived (on by default) ---   calc_density_metrics      = TRUE,   calc_distribution_indices = TRUE,   calc_advanced_metrics     = TRUE,    # --- extended (off by default — enable as needed) ---   calc_diameter_quantiles = TRUE,   # 90th/95th/99th percentile + modal peaks   calc_color_metrics      = TRUE,   # RGB colour per root vs background pixels   calc_root_angles        = TRUE,   # deep_drive + steepness angle distribution   calc_root_order_metrics = TRUE,   # branching order (main vs lateral roots)   calc_landscape_metrics  = FALSE   # slow — enable only when needed )

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: calc_diameter_quantiles = TRUE, can specify custom diameter thresholds separate fine roots coarse roots:
code: result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg                = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl                = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   calc_diameter_quantiles = TRUE,   diameter_thresholds     = c(0.2, 0.5, 1.0),   # fine / medium / coarse   diameter_threshold_unit = "mm"                 # "mm", "cm", or "px" ) # Resulting columns include: rootlength.above.0.2mm, rootlength.above.0.5mm, ...

what: Root branching order (main vs. lateral roots)
title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: calc_root_order_metrics = TRUE builds segment graph skeleton image (via branch_order_map()) classifies every root segment branch order — thickest, central root connected component order 1 (“main” axis), laterals order 2, laterals order 3, . See Minirhizotron Scans vignette worked example underlying pipeline. adds two kinds columns: Per depth bin — branching order varies depth: Per tube (repeated every row tube) — main root vs. lateral roots, split order_metrics(..., focal = "thickest"): Slow. builds one segment graph per image, considerably expensive metric groups. Disable (default) quick exploratory runs enable need architectural detail.
code: result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg                = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl                = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   session                 = "2022_02",   calc_root_order_metrics = TRUE ) library(ggplot2)  ggplot(result, aes(x = depth, y = lateral_root_fraction, colour = Tube)) +   geom_line() +   geom_point() +   coord_flip() +   scale_x_reverse() +   theme_minimal() +   labs(     title = "Lateral Root Fraction by Depth",     x     = "Soil Depth (cm)",     y     = "Fraction of skeleton pixels with branch order > 1"   )

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: glass-fronted rhizotron boxes (flat geometry, tube curvature), set flat_geometry = TRUE. disables sinusoidal depth correction otherwise applied cylindrical minirhizotron tubes.
code: result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg      = "scans/segmented/rhizotron_A/",   path.skl      = "scans/skeleton/rhizotron_A/",   flat_geometry = TRUE,   insertion_angles = 0   # vertical windows )

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: skeleton RGB directories contain files segmented directory (e.g. multiple sessions one folder), use *_file_index select matching subset:
code: # Use only files 37–72 from the RGB directory result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg       = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl       = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   path.rgb       = "scans/blended/all_sessions/",   rgb_file_index = 37:72,   session        = "2022_02" )

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: Pass output_path save result .RData file (object named root.depth.metrics inside file). directory created exist.
code: result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg    = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl    = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   session     = "2022_02",   output_path = "output/root_metrics_2022_02.RData" )  # Reload later: # load("output/root_metrics_2022_02.RData") # head(root.depth.metrics)

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: default (verbose = TRUE) function prints progress line image showing per-image time, cumulative elapsed time, estimated remaining time, predicted clock-time completion: metric block fails single image (e.g. corrupted skeleton file), skipped [RootScanR] SKIPPED message corresponding columns filled NA. Processing always continues. Images loaded skipped entirely listed warning end. suppress progress output:
code: [RootScanR] [3/48] T042 | img: 12s | elapsed: 38s | remaining: ~546s | done ~14:22 result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   verbose  = FALSE )

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
code: library(ggplot2)  # Root length density by depth, one line per tube ggplot(result, aes(x = depth, y = rootlength.density, colour = Tube)) +   geom_line() +   geom_point() +   coord_flip() +   scale_x_reverse() +   theme_minimal() +   labs(     title = "Root Length Density by Depth",     x     = "Soil Depth (cm)",     y     = "Root Length Density (cm root / cm² area)"   )  # Average root diameter by depth ggplot(result, aes(x = depth, y = avg.diameter)) +   geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE) +   coord_flip() +   scale_x_reverse() +   theme_minimal() +   labs(     title = "Average Root Diameter with Depth",     x     = "Soil Depth (cm)",     y     = "Average Diameter (cm)"   )  # mrd distribution across tubes ggplot(result |> dplyr::distinct(Tube, mrd), aes(x = mrd)) +   geom_histogram(bins = 15, fill = "steelblue4", colour = "white") +   theme_minimal() +   labs(     title = "Mean Root Depth Distribution (MRD)",     x     = "MRD",     y     = "Count"   )

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
code: tube_meta <- read.csv("metadata/tube_metadata_2022_02.csv")  result <- root_depth_metrics(    # Paths   path.seg       = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl       = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   path.rgb       = "scans/blended/2022_02/",    # Per-image metadata   insertion_angles = tube_meta$angle,   soil_starts      = tube_meta$soil_row,   tube_names       = tube_meta$tube_id,   session          = "2022_02",    # Geometry   dpi               = 300,   tube_diameter_cm  = 7,   depth_interval_cm = 5,   flat_geometry     = FALSE,    # Core metrics   calc_root_pixels    = TRUE,   calc_root_length    = TRUE,   calc_diameter_stats = TRUE,    # Extended metrics   calc_diameter_quantiles = TRUE,   calc_color_metrics      = TRUE,   calc_root_angles        = TRUE,   calc_root_order_metrics = TRUE,    # main vs. lateral root architecture   calc_landscape_metrics  = FALSE,   # enable if you need patch metrics    # Derived metrics   calc_density_metrics      = TRUE,   calc_distribution_indices = TRUE,   calc_advanced_metrics     = TRUE,    # Diameter thresholds (for quantile block)   diameter_thresholds     = c(0.2, 0.5, 1.0),   diameter_threshold_unit = "mm",    # Output   output_path = "output/root_metrics_2022_02.RData",   verbose     = TRUE )

title: Analyzing Root Traits from Flatbed Scans with RootScanR in R
text: Flatbed scanning captures root morphology 2D plane high resolution controlled lighting. Unlike minirhizotron images, flatbed scans depth dimension — full root system visible traits computed globally rather per depth bin. vignette covers individual-function workflow flatbed images. working minirhizotron tubes want single-call batch approach, see Batch Processing vignette instead. Prerequisite: RootScanR works already-segmented images (root = 1, background = 0). Segmentation must done beforehand using RootDetector RootPainter.

title: Analyzing Root Traits from Flatbed Scans with RootScanR in R
code: # install.packages("remotes") # remotes::install_github("jcunow/RootScanR")  library(RootScanR) library(terra) library(tidyverse)

title: Analyzing Root Traits from Flatbed Scans with RootScanR in R
text: load_flexible_image() accepts rasters, arrays, file paths, common image formats. scale argument controls value rescaling: "binary" forces 0/1, "to_01" maps 0-255 0-1, "to_255" maps 0-1 0-255, "none" leaves values untouched. load files: vignette use bundled example flatbed scan every step runs. 3-band segmentation; layer 2 root channel.
code: # Segmented image (binary: root = 1, background = 0) seg <- load_flexible_image(   "path/to/segmented_scan.tif",   output_format = "spatrast",   scale         = "binary",   select.layer  = 2        # RootDetector: layer 2 is the root channel )  # Original RGB scan for colour analysis rgb <- load_flexible_image(   "path/to/rgb_scan.tif",   output_format = "spatrast",   scale         = "none" ) data(flatbed_scan_example) seg <- terra::rast(flatbed_scan_example) seg <- terra::ifel(seg[[2]] > 0, 1, 0)   # binarized root channel (0/1)  terra::plot(seg, main = "Segmented flatbed scan")

text: branch_order_map() goes one step detect_skeleton_points(): builds full segment graph skeleton classifies every segment branch order — thickest, central root order 1 (main axis), laterals order 2, laterals order 3, . flatbed scans depth dimension, computed whole image. order_metrics() can split architecture main root(s) versus laterals (selected diameter, works regardless many order classes found): order_metrics() also accepts numeric focal split one specific order class rest: check classification visually, write colour-coded overlay PNG, re-plot sub-window native resolution QC: control crossing resolution, pruning weak tips, continuation rule used group segments roots, see ?root_graph_pipeline (engine behind branch_order_map()).
code: order_res <- branch_order_map(   skel  = skl,    # skeleton from step 2   mask  = seg,    # filled root mask, same grid — used for diameters   order = "branch_order",   unit  = "cm",   dpi   = 300 )  # One row per order class: total length, mean diameter, tips, branch points order_res$summary  # Rasterised branch-order classes, aligned to the skeleton plot(order_res$class_map, main = "Branch order") main_vs_lateral <- order_metrics(order_res, focal = "thickest") print(main_vs_lateral)  # Fraction of total root length that is lateral (order >= 2) lateral_length_fraction <- main_vs_lateral$length_fraction[main_vs_lateral$group == "rest"] # Per-order-class table (same as order_res$summary) order_metrics(order_res)  # Order 1 (main root) vs. everything else order_metrics(order_res, focal = 1) order_res2 <- branch_order_map(   skel          = skl,   mask          = seg,   order         = "branch_order",   unit          = "cm",   dpi           = 300,   overlay_png   = "branch_order_overlay.png",   keep_segments = TRUE )  # Zoom into a 200x200 px window at 3x magnification plot_order_window(   order_res2$edges, skl,   r_range = c(100, 300), c_range = c(100, 300),   scale = 3, file = "branch_order_window.png" )

text: create_depthmap() assigns soil depth value (cm) every pixel based tube geometry (diameter, tilt angle, DPI) sinusoidal correction curvature cylindrical tubes. Important parameters:  start.soil: accurate depth attribution requires knowing soil surface image. -situ calibration (marking tube installation) reliable approach. code: # `mask` flags foreign objects (e.g. tube edges / tape) to exclude from depth # attribution. Here it is derived from the RootDetector layers. mask <- seg_img[[1]] - seg_img[[2]] mask[mask == 255] <- NA  depth_map <- create_depthmap(   img           = seg_img,   mask          = mask,   sinoid        = TRUE,   tube.thicc    = 7,       # cm — measure your own tube   tilt          = 45,      # degrees   dpi           = 150,   start.soil    = 2.9,     # cm — from in-situ calibration   center.offset = 0.5      # 0 = top of tube, 1 = bottom )  # create_depthmap() returns the map on its own grid; align it to the segmented # image so depth and root rasters share extent/resolution (required for # terra::zonal() and zoning() below). depth_map <- terra::flip(terra::t(depth_map)) terra::ext(depth_map) <- terra::ext(root_layer)  terra::plot(depth_map, main = "Depth map (cm)")

code: # Align RGB to the segmented grid (skip if they already match) rgb_aligned <- terra::resample(rgb, root_layer, method = "bilinear")  colour_list <- lapply(depths, function(d) {   slice_seg <- zoning(root_layer,  mode = "depth", depth_map = depth_bins, depth = d)   slice_rgb <- zoning(rgb_aligned, mode = "depth", depth_map = depth_bins, depth = d)    # Split the slice into root vs. background pixels   root_rgb <- slice_rgb; root_rgb[slice_seg == 0] <- NA   bg_rgb   <- slice_rgb; bg_rgb[slice_seg == 1]   <- NA    # Skip depth bins that contain no roots (nothing to colour)   if (all(is.na(terra::values(root_rgb[[1]], mat = FALSE)))) return(NULL)    data.frame(     depth = d,     setNames(tube_coloration(root_rgb), paste0(names(tube_coloration(root_rgb)), "_root")),     setNames(tube_coloration(bg_rgb),   paste0(names(tube_coloration(bg_rgb)),   "_bg"))   ) }) colour_df <- do.call(rbind, colour_list)   # NULLs from empty bins are dropped

text: tube-level summary metrics require complete depth profile. root_accumulation() complements returning cumulative profile — useful plotting root mass/length accumulates depth, comparing shape accumulation curves tubes/plots.  stdrz = "relative" rescales cumulative curve end 1, makes accumulation curves directly comparable tubes differ total root abundance — shape depth distribution compared.
code: # Mean rooting depth — depth-weighted mean mrd_val <- MRD(w = depth_data$depth, roots = depth_data$rootlength.density)  # Root Penetration Index — how evenly roots are distributed with depth (-1 to 1) rpi_val  <- RPI(roots = depth_data$rootlength.density, w = depth_data$depth)  # Total length density — sum over the full profile total_ld <- sum(depth_data$rootlength.density * 5, na.rm = TRUE)  cat(sprintf("MRD: %.2f  RPI: %.3f  Total length density: %.4f\n",             mrd_val, rpi_val, total_ld)) #> MRD: 19.47  RPI: 0.899  Total length density: 43.6782 # Cumulative root pixel count with depth, per tube depth_data$Tube <- "T067"   # add a grouping column if not already present  depth_data$rootpx.cumulative <- root_accumulation(   depth_data,   group    = "Tube",   depth    = "depth",   variable = "rootpx",   stdrz    = "relative"   # "counts" (raw), "additive" (/max), or "relative" (/sum) )  plot(depth_data$depth, depth_data$rootpx.cumulative, type = "l",      xlab = "Depth (cm)", ylab = "Cumulative root pixel fraction",      main = "Root accumulation with depth")

title: Rotation Bias and Rhythmicity Analysis with RootScanR
text: vignette demonstrates detect correct rotational bias minirhizotron setups using RootScanR package. minirhizotron studies, scanner tube inserted angle soil can rotate slightly sessions. CI-600 similar scanners cover full 360° arc, sequential images tube may perfectly overlap. Left uncorrected, rotation introduces systematic spatial bias — roots near scan edges observed sessions others. RootScanR addresses four functions: estimate_rotation_center() — locates rotational zero point tape coverage single image estimate_rotation_shift() — quantifies pixel offset two sessions using image correlation rotation_censor() — crops images shared, overlap region zoning(mode = "rotation") — splits tube surface slices along rotation axis (circumference), root traits can summarised separately slice last point matters beyond rotation correction : tube circumference split slices, rhythmicity() / fit_sine_curve() family — normally introduced time-series data — can applied sequence per-slice root traits test whether roots distributed evenly around tube circumference, whether systematic top-/ side--side pattern (e.g. roots underside tube). spatial, temporal, application functions — see Circumferential zoning rhythmicity section .

what: 2. Estimate the rotation shift between two sessions
title: Rotation Bias and Rhythmicity Analysis with RootScanR
text: tube scanned two different sessions, scanner may rotated. estimate_rotation_shift() uses either cross-correlation ("ccf") phase correlation ("phase") shared depth window find pixel offset. Phase correlation generally robust brightness differences sessions.  returned vector c(x.lag, y.lag) — horizontal (depth axis) vertical (rotation axis) pixel shifts. large vertical shift means tube rotated substantially sessions. Planned: single helper estimates estimate_rotation_shift() two sessions applies rotation_censor() using shift directly, aligning session coverage becomes one-step operation. , use two functions shown steps 2 3.

what: 3. Censor image edges to the shared overlap region
title: Rotation Bias and Rhythmicity Analysis with RootScanR
text: rotation_censor() crops image rows present every session retained. eliminates non-overlapping margins makes root counts directly comparable across sessions. Two modes available: fixed.rotation = FALSE — cuts proportionally based measured offset; output width varies fixed.rotation = TRUE — centres crop specified row forces fixed output width; recommended comparing multiple sessions Note tube geometry. inner outer tube diameters differ, observed root length slightly underestimates true root length soil. resize coefficient may applied separately; rotation_censor() handle correction.
code: data(seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067) img <- terra::rast(seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067)  # rotation centre (absolute row) to centre the crop on r0 <- estimate_rotation_center(img)  # preview the crop (green = kept, red = cut) and apply it in one call. # fixed.width must be <= image height (1144 rows here) to sit symmetrically. censored <- rotation_censor(   img,   center.offset  = r0,   cut.buffer     = 0.02,   fixed.width    = 800,   fixed.rotation = TRUE,   overlay        = TRUE,   main = "Rotation censor: kept window (green) vs cut (red)" )

code: data(seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067)  root_layer <- load_flexible_image(seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067, scale = "binary", select.layer = 2,                                   output_format = "spatrast")   # rotation trim: drop the top 33% of rows e <- terra::ext(root_layer) root_layer <- terra::crop(root_layer, terra::ext(e[1]+ e[2] / 4, e[2], e[3], e[4]))  plot(root_layer) # --> the trimming avoids noisy deep layer and tape artefacts at the top  # create circumferential zones n_slices <- 48 slices <- slice_rotation(root_layer, n_slices)  # tallies from the binary mask: roots = 1, soil/background = 0 n_root <- function(s) { v <- terra::values(s, mat = FALSE); sum(v > 0,  na.rm = TRUE) } n_soil <- function(s) { v <- terra::values(s, mat = FALSE); sum(v == 0, na.rm = TRUE) }  slice_traits <- data.frame(   slice   = seq_len(n_slices),   rootlen = sapply(slices, root_length, unit = "cm", dpi = 150),  # cm   rootpx  = sapply(slices, n_root),   soilpx  = sapply(slices, n_soil) )  # root length per observed area (root + soil pixels) slice_traits$rld <- slice_traits$rootlen /                     ((slice_traits$rootpx + slice_traits$soilpx) * (2.54/300)^2) #slice_traits$rld.fraction <- slice_traits$rld / sum(slice_traits$rld, na.rm = TRUE)

text: create_root_buffer() grows buffer chosen width around every root pixel – simple proxy rhizosphere / diffusion zone. halo.= TRUE returns ring around roots (excluding roots ).    kernel argument switches "circle" (8-neighbour) "diamond" (4-neighbour) growth shape, width controls many dilation iterations applied. Set halo.= FALSE return roots plus buffer single filled mask.
code: seg  <- load_flexible_image(seg_Oulanka2023_Session03_T067, select.layer = 2) halo <- create_root_buffer(seg, width = 3, halo.only = TRUE, kernel = "circle") terra::plot(halo) halo10 <- create_root_buffer(seg, width = 10, halo.only = TRUE, kernel = "circle") terra::plot(halo10) halo10 <- create_root_buffer(seg, width = 10, halo.only = FALSE, kernel = "circle") terra::plot(halo10)

text: root_turnover() quantifies much root material produced, lost, retained. method selects one two approaches: "tc" (Temporal Comparison) – compare two segmented images different sessions. tc.method argument picks root amount measured: "kimura" (root length) "rootpx" (root pixel count). "dpc" (Decay, Production, Constant) – decompose single multi-layer image produced RootDetector decayed, newly produced, unchanged root fractions. bundled TurnoverDPC_data DPC-style image: two-timepoint comparison instead pass sessions:
code: dpc <- terra::rast(TurnoverDPC_data) root_turnover(dpc, method = "dpc") #>     tape constant production   decay newgrowth.ratio decay.ratio constant.ratio #> 1 887012   438681    2770138 3355722          0.8633      0.8844         0.0668 s1 <- terra::rast(skl_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067) s3 <- terra::rast(skl_Oulanka2023_Session03_T067) root_turnover(s1, s3, method = "tc", tc.method = "rootpx", unit = "cm", dpi = 150, select.layer = 2)

text: consecutive pair frames (left) B (right) within tube, wrapper: Edge band — takes vertical band (rows vertical_offset vertical_offset + vertical_region) ’s right edge_width columns B’s left edge_width columns. Reduce & preprocess — converts band luma applies preprocess set. Align — computes FFT phase correlation two bands; peak gives shift (dx, dy) confidence peak. what: Demo: slice a real scan and stitch it back
title: Stitching Scan Sequences into Mosaics with RootScanR
text: rgb_Oulanka2023_Session03_T067 real RGB tube scan. cut overlapping pieces, save little “tube” folder, let stitch_root_scans() reassemble — exactly path scans take.  recovered dx ~0 (sliced identical edges) peak high — clean alignment — two panels indistinguishable. (chunk needs suggested png package write demo files.)
code: data("rgb_Oulanka2023_Session03_T067")  # load to an (H, W, C) array; downsampled here only to keep the vignette light img <- load_flexible_image(terra::rast(rgb_Oulanka2023_Session03_T067),                            output_format = "array", normalize = FALSE) #> Warning: `normalize`, `binarize`, and `denormalize` are deprecated; use `scale` #> ("to_01", "to_255", "binary", "none") instead. img <- img[seq(1, nrow(img), 3), seq(1, ncol(img), 3), , drop = FALSE] dim(img)                                   # rows (rotation) x cols (length) x RGB #> [1]  387 1633    3 W       <- ncol(img) piece_w <- W %/% 3L                        # each piece ~1/3 of the length ov      <- piece_w %/% 4L                  # overlap = 25% of a piece step    <- piece_w - ov starts  <- (0:((W - piece_w) %/% step)) * step pieces  <- lapply(starts, function(s) img[, (s + 1):(s + piece_w), , drop = FALSE]) length(pieces)                             # number of overlapping frames #> [1] 3 # save the pieces as one tube ("T067") in a temp folder, as if freshly scanned d  <- file.path(tempdir(), "stitch_demo") dir.create(d, showWarnings = FALSE) sc <- if (max(img, na.rm = TRUE) > 1) 255 else 1 for (i in seq_along(pieces)) {   png::writePNG(pmin(pmax(pieces[[i]] / sc, 0), 1),                 file.path(d, sprintf("Oulanka_T067_%02d.png", i))) } list.files(d) #> [1] "Oulanka_T067_01.png" "Oulanka_T067_02.png" "Oulanka_T067_03.png" res <- stitch_root_scans(d, pattern = ".png", edge_width = ov,                          vertical_region = nrow(img), vertical_offset = 0,                          report = TRUE, verbose = FALSE) mosaic <- res$mosaics[["T067"]] dim(mosaic)            # reconstructed length #> [1]  387 1360    3 res$report             # per-join dx, dy, peak (confidence), overlap #>   tube step dx dy peak overlap #> 1 T067    1  0  0    1     136 #> 2 T067    2  0  0    1     136 norm01 <- function(a) a / max(a, na.rm = TRUE)         # display only span   <- ncol(mosaic)  op <- graphics::par(mfrow = c(2, 1), mar = c(0, 0, 1.4, 0)) plot(grDevices::as.raster(norm01(img[, seq_len(span), , drop = FALSE]))) graphics::title("original scan (cropped to the stitched span)") plot(grDevices::as.raster(norm01(mosaic))) graphics::title("re-stitched by stitch_root_scans()") graphics::par(op)

title: Stitching Scan Sequences into Mosaics with RootScanR
text: setting maps onto step . edge_width — band width used align. overlap = edge_width - dx, set roughly 1-2x true overlap (overlap half edge_width). small misses matching content; large dilutes peak. vertical_region / vertical_offset — rows matched. Raise vertical_offset skip header, label, tape strip top. direction — "horizontal" (default, frames side--side) "vertical" (frames stacked top--bottom, e.g. strips acquired tube). method — "phase" (FFT phase correlation) supported. Python tool’s "feature" (SIFT/ORB) needs OpenCV CRAN backend, errors message rather guessing. preprocess — applied edge bands correlation: blend / blend_width — overlap combined: segmented masks (root = 1, background = 0) prefer "max": feathering averages, turning root pixel fractional value (e.g. 0.5) ghosting thin roots alignment pixel. "max" keeps root either frame never invents -values. blend_width narrows linear ramp centred band reduce ghosting colour scans.

title: Stitching Scan Sequences into Mosaics with RootScanR
text: default frames stitched sorted file-name order (see Naming, selection & ordering change ). stitch_root_scans() also accepts plain character vector paths.
code: # preview first list_tubes("path/to/scans", pattern = ".tiff")        # index | tube | n_frames  # stitch every tube, one PNG each stitch_root_scans("path/to/scans", pattern = ".tiff", out_dir = "path/to/output")  # stitch a subset, pick a blend for segmented masks, and get a quality report res <- stitch_root_scans(   "path/to/scans", pattern = ".tiff",   tubes  = 1:36,           # or tubes = c("T037","T040"), or tubes = "ask"   blend  = "max",   report = TRUE ) res$mosaics                                  # named list, one array per tube res$report                                   # tube | step | dx | dy | peak | overlap aggregate(peak ~ tube, res$report, mean)     # mean confidence per tube

title: Stitching Scan Sequences into Mosaics with RootScanR
text: Grouping — group_regex. regular expression pulls tube id path; files sharing id form one sequence. default "T0\\d{2}" (\\ R string-escaping regex T0\d{2}) matches T0 plus two digits, e.g. T067. Adapt names, use NULL put every file single mosaic: Selecting — select vs tubes. select indexes sorted file list grouping; tubes selects whole tubes index, name, interactively. Use list_scan_files() / list_tubes() see indices. Normally want tubes; select raw file ranges. Ordering — order_by / decreasing. Frames within tube stitched order. default key file name, sorts lexicographically — unpadded numbers come wrong (_10 _2). Use order_by sort frame number instead, decreasing = TRUE reverse (e.g. frames numbered bottom tube upward): (Equivalently, zero-pad frame numbers — _02, _10 — default name sort already correct.) Output names — out_prefix / out_format. out_dir set, mosaic written <out_dir>/<out_prefix><tube>.<ext>. out_prefix tags session/site separate runs don’t collide; out_format picks "png" (default) "tiff":
code: group_regex = "T\\d+"          # T1, T67, T123 group_regex = "tube[-_]?\\d+"  # tube12 / tube_12 / tube-12 group_regex = NULL             # no grouping -> one mosaic stitch_root_scans("scans", pattern = ".tiff", select = 1:36)              # first 36 files stitch_root_scans("scans", pattern = ".tiff", tubes  = 1:36)              # first 36 tubes stitch_root_scans("scans", pattern = ".tiff", tubes  = c("T037", "T040")) # named tubes stitch_root_scans("scans", pattern = ".tiff", tubes  = "ask")             # interactive # order by the number just before the extension, e.g. ..._02.tiff, ..._10.tiff stitch_root_scans("scans", pattern = ".tiff", order_by = "\\d+(?=\\.)") stitch_root_scans("scans", pattern = ".tiff", order_by = "\\d+(?=\\.)", decreasing = TRUE) stitch_root_scans("scans/Oulanka2020_November", pattern = ".tiff",                   out_dir = "mosaics", out_prefix = "Oulanka2020_Session03_",                   out_format = "tiff") # -> mosaics/Oulanka2020_Session03_T067.tif, ...T068.tif, ...

code: # Load example flatbed scan library(RootScanR) library(terra) data("seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067") img <- seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067  # Preprocess and extract traits binary <- load_flexible_image(img) cleaned <- clean_image(binary, max_artifact_size = 5, max_hole_size = 5, select.layer = 2) skel <- skeletonize_image(cleaned,  methods = "GuoHall", verbose = F)  # Length and diameter root_length(skel, unit = "cm", dpi = 150) diam_map <- root_diameter(cleaned, unit = "cm", dpi = 150, select.layer = NULL ) modal_peaks(diam_map$diameters, prominence_threshold = 10, mclust = F, adjust = 5, display_type = "density")   # Architecture metrics <- detect_skeleton_points(skel) print(metrics)  # Branch order: main axis (order 1) vs. lateral roots (order >= 2) order_res <- branch_order_map(skel, mask = cleaned, order = "branch_order", unit = "cm", dpi = 150) order_metrics(order_res, focal = "thickest")  # Visualize terra::plot(diam_map$diameter_rast) terra::plot(skel)

Required skel NULL. order scheme labels $class_map/$summary: "branch_order", "root_order", "tip_order". unit Reporting unit: "cm", "inch", "px". dpi Scan resolution (dots per inch); required cm/inch. length_method "polyline" (sqrt(2) chain code, follows curves) "kimura" (per-segment Kimura correction, better straight segments). template SpatRaster defining $class_map grid; defaults skel raster. overlay_png Optional path; writes order-coloured validation PNG. return_map Build $class_map. ... title: Branch-order classification of a root skeleton — branch_order_map

title: Branch-order classification of a root skeleton — branch_order_map
text: three order schemes always computed stored $edges; order selects labels $class_map $summary. tip_order (per segment) Topological leaf-peeling (Strahler-like).     Every terminal segment order 1; peeling terminals away round round,     segment's order 1 + max(child orders). Order rises toward     interior, distal end even thick root 1. root_order (per root) Segments grouped continuous roots     (continuation rule ); root takes maximum tip_order     along , thick main axis keeps high order tip. Sensitive     deep deepest subtree runs. branch_order (per root) Centrifugal generation. thickest root     (length-weighted mean diameter) connected component order 1;     counting branching hops outward, laterals 2, 3, .     Independent subtree depth, one heavily branched root inflate     rest. Continuation rule (groups segments roots root_order branch_order): junction two arms forming root chosen straightness + diam_weight * diameter_similarity. diam_weight = 0 uses angle ; larger values let thickness dominate. odd arm lateral. tip_order use rule.

title: Clean a binary root image — clean_image
text: Performs three sequential cleaning operations binary segmented image: 1. **Hole filling** – fills black regions enclosed white (segmentation    gaps inside roots). 2. **Artifact removal** — removes isolated white specks connected    image border (false-positive root detections). 3. **Edge smoothing** *(optional, default)* – applies morphological    closing smooth jagged root edges.

title: Clean a binary root image — clean_image
code: clean_image(   img,   pre_threshold = NULL,   pre_threshold_method = "global",   pre_threshold_window_size = 15,   max_hole_size = NULL,   max_artifact_size = NULL,   edge_smooth = FALSE,   kernel_shape = "disk",   kernel_size = 3,   iterations = 1,   select.layer = NULL,   output_format = "spatrast",   report = FALSE )

Default `15`. max_hole_size Maximum hole size pixels fill.  `NULL`, enclosed holes filled.  See **Choosing thresholds** . max_artifact_size Maximum artifact size pixels remove.  `NULL`, isolated white regions removed. edge_smooth Logical.  Apply morphological closing hole/artifact cleaning.  Default `FALSE`. kernel_shape Structuring element shape edge smoothing: `"disk"` (default), `"square"`, `"diamond"`. kernel_size Structuring element size (odd integer).  Default `3`. iterations Number closing iterations edge smoothing. Default `1`. select.layer Integer `NULL`.  layer use multi-layer inputs. output_format Character.  Format returned object.  One `"spatrast"` (default), `"cimg"`, `"matrix"`.  Using `"spatrast"` means result can passed directly `terra::plot()`, `skeletonize_image()`, `root_length()`, etc. without conversion. report Logical.  `TRUE`, also calls [report_image_components()] *original* (uncleaned) image cleaning.  `output_format = "spatrast"` (default), cleaned raster returned directly even `report = TRUE`; report printed side effect. title: Clean a binary root image — clean_image
text: `skeletonize_image()` uses Medial Axis Transform, driven distance transform.  Small holes inside root inflate local distance values force medial axis bifurcate around hole, producing spurious branching points.  Isolated artifact pixels produce phantom skeleton segments.  Cleaning first yields much cleaner skeleton.

title: Detect endpoints and branching points in a skeleton image — detect_skeleton_points
text: computation proceeds follows: 1. Raster values converted numeric matrix. 2. 1-pixel zero-padding border added around matrix. 3. pixel original image:    - 3x3 window extracted padded matrix    - center pixel excluded    - Neighbor count computed sum remaining 8 values Classification rules: - Endpoint: pixel == 1 neighbor count == 1 - Branch point: pixel == 1 neighbor count > 2 Outputs converted back SpatRaster objects.

title: Zhang-Suen thinning using lookup table (LUT implementation) — lut_thin_fast
text: algorithm operates follows: 1. Raster values extracted numeric vector. 2. iteration reconstructs matrix representation image. 3. zero-padded border added around image. 4. pixel equal 1:    - 3x3 neighbourhood extracted    - weighted sum (mask encoding) produces unique neighbourhood code 5. code used index 256-entry lookup table:    - LUT value 1 3 -> pixel removed first sub-step    - LUT value 2 3 -> pixel removed second sub-step 6. Pixels updated -place vector representation 7. Iteration stops pixels removed max_iter reached Important properties implementation: - Raster written back end - intermediate steps operate -memory matrix/vector data - Neighborhood encoding performed via explicit loops image pixels

text: path.seg Character. Path directory binary segmented images (foreground/root pixel = 1, background = 0). path.skl Character NULL. Path directory skeletonised images (one-pixel-wide centrelines roots), used length, diameter, angle, branching-order metrics. NULL file missing, skeleton computed internally via skeletonize_image(). Default NULL. path.rgb Character NULL. Path directory blended RGB images, aligned segmented images.  Required colour metrics. Default NULL. seg_file_index Integer vector NULL. Optional subset index applied list.files(path.seg), e.g. 37:72.  Default NULL (use files). skl_file_index Integer vector NULL. Optional subset index applied list.files(path.skl).  Default NULL. rgb_file_index Integer vector NULL. Optional subset index applied list.files(path.rgb).  Default NULL. insertion_angles Numeric. Insertion angle tube window vertical, degrees.  0 = perfectly vertical, 30 = tilted 30 degrees vertical (common minirhizotron angle). Used create_depthmap() correct depth scale. Default 0. soil_starts Numeric. Pixel row (original, un-rotated image) soil surface begins.  Used set zero-depth reference. Default 0. tube_names Character NULL. Sample/tube identifiers added Tube column.  NULL, names derived characters 3-5 right segmented file name, prefixed "T" (e.g. "T042").  Adjust naming convention differs. Default NULL. session Character. Session campaign label added Session column, e.g. "2022_02".  Default "". dpi Numeric. Scanner resolution dots per inch.  Used convert pixel distances physical units (cm).  Default 300. tube_diameter_cm Numeric. Inner diameter minirhizotron tube centimetres.  Passed create_depthmap() tube.thicc.  role flat-window geometry (flat_geometry = TRUE) uncertain; likely default inside create_depthmap() may ignored – leave default unless know matters setup.  Default 7. depth_interval_cm Numeric. Size depth bin centimetres.  Passed nn binning(). Default 5. flat_geometry Logical.  FALSE (default), images treated cylindrical minirhizotron tubes sinusoidal depth correction applied (sinoid = TRUE create_depthmap()).  Set TRUE flat rhizotron windows (e.g. glass-fronted boxes) sinusoidal correction needed (sinoid = FALSE).  Default FALSE. calc_root_pixels Logical. Count rootpx (foreground pixels) voidpx (background pixels) per depth bin.  Required density metrics.  Default TRUE. calc_root_length Logical. Estimate root length (cm) per depth bin skeleton using D8 connectivity (orthogonal steps = 1 pixel, diagonal steps = \(\sqrt{2}\) pixels, isolated pixels = 1 pixel). Required length density angle metrics.  Default TRUE. calc_diameter_stats Logical. Compute per-bin mean, maximum, variance root diameter (cm) using distance-transform approach root_diameter().  Default TRUE. calc_diameter_quantiles Logical. Compute diameter distribution percentiles set diameter_quantiles per bin, conditional means quantile, threshold-based root lengths (see diameter_thresholds), modal diameter peaks via modal_peaks().  Default FALSE. calc_landscape_metrics Logical. Compute patch-level landscape metrics per depth bin via root_scape_metrics(): nearest-neighbour distance (enn_mn), joint entropy (joinent), relative mutual information (relmutinf), number patches (np), contagion (contag).  Slow: one call per depth bin per image.  Default FALSE. calc_color_metrics Logical. Compute mean chromatic coordinates (rcc, gcc, bcc), hue, saturation, luminosity, raw RGB channel means separately root pixels background pixels via tube_coloration().  Requires path.rgb.  Default FALSE. calc_root_angles Logical. Compute deep_drive (fraction skeleton pixels whose D8 flow direction matches locally optimal downward direction) mean.steepness.angle / sd.steepness.angle (degrees, 0 = horizontal, 90 = vertical). Uses deep_drive().  Default FALSE. calc_root_order_metrics Logical. Build per-image branching-order graph via branch_order_map() summarise per depth bin per tube.  Adds mean.branch_order, max.branch_order, mean.root_order, lateral_root_fraction per depth bin, plus tube-level main_root.* / lateral_roots.* columns (length, diameter, branching frequency, etc., split order_metrics(..., focal = "thickest")) n_root_orders (highest branch order found).  Requires skeleton.  Slow: builds one segment graph per image.  Default FALSE. calc_density_metrics Logical. Compute rootpx.density (percent root area cover) rootlength.density (cm root length per cm^2 imaged area) per bin.  Auto-enables calc_root_pixels calc_root_length.  Default TRUE. calc_distribution_indices Logical. Compute tube-level indices: mrd (mean rooting depth), rpi (root penetration index), total.length.density (summed length density bins, cm root per cm^2 per cm depth).  Auto-enables calc_density_metrics.  Default TRUE. calc_advanced_metrics Logical. Compute per-bin derived metrics: rootlength.fraction (bin's length density fraction tube total), mean.var.diameter (mean within-bin diameter variance), rootsurface_rootvolume_ratio (lateral surface area cylinder volume, summed skeleton pixels bin expressed cm^2 per cm^3).  Auto-enables calc_distribution_indices calc_diameter_stats.  Default TRUE. diameter_thresholds Numeric vector. Diameter cut-offs computing rootlength..* avg.diameter..* columns. title: Estimate Root Diameters — root_diameter
text: function works follows: - Converts input image binary format (`cimg`). - Applies distance transform compute Euclidean distance foreground (root) pixels. - Skeletonizes binary image identify root centerlines. - Filters distance values retain corresponding skeletonized regions. - Computes diameter statistics, including quantiles, mean, median diameters. function supports various input formats normalizes image values range [0, 1] needed. uses `terra` package raster operations `imager` package image processing.

Summary statistics: #> Mean diameter: 2.23 #> Median diameter: 2.00 #> Number of valid measurements: 78956  # Access results: print(result$mean_diameter) #> [1] 2.233119 terra::plot(result$skeleton_rast)

title: Root length estimation from skeleton images — root_length
text: Root length pixels converted units

title: Root length estimation from skeleton images — root_length
text: Let Nd number diagonal pixel connections number orthogonal pixel connections skeleton. Freeman methods treat skeleton discrete chain-code path: - freeman_basic:   L = sqrt(2) * Nd + - freeman_corrected:   L = 0.948 * (sqrt(2) * Nd + ) Kimura methods treat skeleton discretized representation underlying continuous curve reduce orientation bias: - kimura1:   L = sqrt(Nd^2 + (Nd + )^2) - kimura2 (default):   L = sqrt(Nd^2 + (Nd + /2)^2) + /2 Kimura2 estimator generally preferred due improved stability across object orientations curvature distributions.

title: Unified Root Turnover Analysis — root_turnover
text: Wrapper around two root-turnover methods. method selects one runs: "tc" (Temporal Comparison) Compares two timepoint images     (img1, img2) reports standing roots, production,     new-root percentages. Dispatches turnover_tc.     tc.method argument chooses root amount measured:     "kimura" (root length) "rootpx" (root pixel count). "dpc" (Decay, Production, Constant) Decomposes single     multi-layer 'RootDetector' image decayed, newly produced,     unchanged (constant) root fractions. Dispatches     turnover_dpc; img2 used.

title: Skeletonize binary image — skeletonize_image

title: Skeletonize binary image — skeletonize_image
text: Processing steps: 1. Input converted single-layer binary SpatRaster using    `load_flexible_image()`. 2. Foreground pixel count computed. 3. Skeletonization performed using `lut_thin_fast()`:    - iterative removal pixels based 3x3 neighbourhood codes    - lookup table determines pixel deletions two sub-steps per iteration 4. Output final thinned binary raster. 5. Optionally, overlay image generated:    - original image marked base layer    - skeleton pixels overlaid separate class    - saved using base R PNG plotting

title: Stitch two scans into one mosaic with linear feather blending — stitch_image_pair
text: img1, img2 Image inputs (file path, SpatRaster, Raster*, cimg, magick-image, matrix, array). See load_flexible_image supported formats. method Alignment method. Currently "phase" (FFT phase correlation) supported. "feature" (SIFT/ORB keypoint matching Python original) requires OpenCV CRAN-compatible R backend; raises informative error. edge_width Width pixels edge band used alignment. Clamped image width. Set close true overlap - roughly 1-2x (overlap half edge_width) - reliable peak. vertical_region Height pixels vertical band used alignment. vertical_offset Starting row (top) vertical band. direction "horizontal" (default, frames side side) "vertical" (frames stacked top bottom, e.g. minirhizotron strips acquired tube). Vertical transposes inputs, runs horizontal core, transposes result back. preprocess Preprocessing applied edge bands correlation: one "none" (default), "center" (subtract mean), "norm" (divide SD), "center_norm", "hann" (demean + Hann window), "grad" (gradient magnitude) "grad_norm". "hann", "grad" "center" help scans uneven lighting sparse texture; "norm" alone barely changes phase correlation (already scale-invariant). well-textured scans "none" usually fine. blend overlap band combined: "linear" (default, alpha ramp 1 -> 0, good colour scans), "overlay" (img2 hides img1), "overlay_first" (img1 hides img2), "max" (lighten / union - recommended segmented/binary masks, averaging make fractional values ghost thin roots) "min" (darken). blend_width Optional width (px) linear ramp, centred overlap (hard img1 left, hard img2 right); NULL (default) ramps across whole overlap. smaller value reduces root ghosting colour scans. Ignored unless blend = "linear".

"feature" (SIFT/ORB keypoint matching Python original) requires OpenCV CRAN-compatible R backend; raises informative error. edge_width Width pixels edge band used alignment. Clamped image width. Set close true overlap - roughly 1-2x (overlap half edge_width) - reliable peak. vertical_region Height pixels vertical band used alignment. vertical_offset Starting row (top) vertical band. direction "horizontal" (default, frames side side) "vertical" (frames stacked top bottom, e.g. minirhizotron strips acquired tube). Vertical transposes inputs, runs horizontal core, transposes result back. preprocess Preprocessing applied edge bands correlation: one "none" (default), "center" (subtract mean), "norm" (divide SD), "center_norm", "hann" (demean + Hann window), "grad" (gradient magnitude) "grad_norm". "hann", "grad" "center" help scans uneven lighting sparse texture; "norm" alone barely changes phase correlation (already scale-invariant). well-textured scans "none" usually fine. blend overlap band combined: "linear" (default, alpha ramp 1 -> 0, good colour scans), "overlay" (img2 hides img1), "overlay_first" (img1 hides img2), "max" (lighten / union - recommended segmented/binary masks, averaging make fractional values ghost thin roots) "min" (darken). blend_width Optional width (px) linear ramp, centred overlap (hard img1 left, hard img2 right); NULL (default) ramps across whole overlap. smaller value reduces root ghosting colour scans. Ignored unless blend = "linear". report Logical. title: Mask a scan to a depth and/or rotation zone (zone masking) — zoning
text: zone defined one : depth (mode = "depth"): keep pixels whose binned     depth (depth_map) matches depth. single value selects     closest available bin; consecutive sequence selects inclusive     range; discrete values select closest bin. rotation (mode = "rotation"): crop contiguous band     circumferential slices. simply splitting tube n equal     slices, prefer slice_rotation; use rotation mode     need combine rotation band depth masking one call     (mode = ""). Note depth masking sets --zone pixels NA (extent unchanged), whereas rotation masking crops extent selected band.

title: Mask a scan to a depth and/or rotation zone (zone masking) — zoning
code: data(seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067) img <- terra::rast(seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067) mask <- img[[1]] - img[[2]]; mask[mask == 255] <- NA depth_map  <- create_depthmap(img, mask, start.soil = 2.9, dpi = 150 ) depth_bins <- binning(depth_map, nn = 5) depth_bins <- terra::flip(terra::t(depth_bins)) # Keep only the root layer pixels that fall in the 10 cm depth bin slice_10cm <- zoning(img[[2]], mode = "depth",                      depth_map = depth_bins, depth = 10)
